content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 25, 2004


Roy A. Hanley, City Attorney
The Municipal Advocates Group, LLP
8930 Morro Road
Atascadero, CA 93422

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. W-04-060

Dear Mr. Hanley:

     This letter is in response to your request for written advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹ Your question concerns Ms. Yaeger's ability to participate in a vote at the planning commission level on whether or not to recommend changes to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang.

     Currently, the same or very similar issues are the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division. Moreover, your question involves analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which may have already occurred, such that issuing further advice at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111. Your request is therefore considered withdrawn.

     Should you have additional questions regarding this matter, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Luisa Menchaca
                                        General Counsel

                                        John W. Wallace
                                        Assistant General Counsel

JWW/jg

________________________
¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 – 18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Ms. Yaeger may participate in a planning commission vote on recommending changes to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same or similar issues, and the question involves analysis of interrelated decisions some of which may have already occurred, making advice on past conduct inconsistent with the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (File No. W-04-060). The document contains no verbatim QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections but clearly identifies the subject matter and reason for withdrawal. The request was withdrawn rather than declined, indicating the FPPC deemed it appropriate to terminate the advice process mid-stream.

QUESTION: Whether Ms. Yaeger may participate in a planning commission vote on recommending changes to the Second Unit Ordinance in the City of Solvang under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for advice because a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division involves the same or similar issues, and the question involves analysis of interrelated decisions some of which may have already occurred, making advice on past conduct inconsistent with the impartial administration of the Political Reform Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC withdrawal letter declining to provide written advice on whether a planning commissioner may vote on changes to a Second Unit Ordinance due to a related pending Enforcement Division investigation and the interrelated nature of past decisions involved.
